title: Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Immigration/Naturalization Legal Services 25 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Domestic Violence Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Human Trafficking Issues Older Adults AMERICAN IMMIGRATION LAWYER ASSOCIATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a search tool to find member immigration lawyers. Lawyers can be located by the type of immigration law practiced, languages spoken, by location, or by last name. Website does not provide legal advice or assistance. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC CHARITIES IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides professional legal representation, advice, and advocacy to low-income immigrants to protect and advance their rights. Focuses on family reunification and assistance to the most vulnerable immigrants, including victims of crimes such as domestic violence and human trafficking. Engages in public education, training, and community outreach to inform immigrants about their rights and options for immigration relief. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard #5 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Low-income immigrants Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm by appointment only Email (503) 231-4866 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES INC CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides civil legal aid and advocacy, can not assist with criminal law cases. Organized into specialty units: Housing/Consumer Unit, Income Maintenance Unit, Employment/Individual Rights Unit (Immigration Law Project, Protecting Our Veterans, and Public Guardian Program), and Family Unit. 225 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Low-income households (125% of the Federal Poverty Level), age 60 and older, and veterans. Specialty units may have additional eligibility requirements. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm (541) 779-7292 Get Directions More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON SOAR PORTLAND SOAR IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal consultations and representation for refugees and immigrants in matters of naturalization, permanent residency, and family petitions, and offers classes to help students prepare for the US Naturalization Test. Services include applications for US citizenship, green cards, family-based visas, Temporary Protected Status (TPS), Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) petitions, U visas, and employment authorization. Through the Cuban-Haitian Program, additional assistance is available for work authorization, parole, adjustment of status, and citizenship. 7931 NE Halsey Street Suite 302 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Individuals who are refugees or immigrants Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 384-2482 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF PORTLAND SOAR HILLSBORO SOAR IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal consultations and representation for refugees and immigrants in matters of naturalization, permanent residency, and family petitions, and offers classes to help students prepare for the US Naturalization Test. Services include applications for US citizenship, green cards, family-based visas, Temporary Protected Status (TPS), Violence Against Women Act (VAWA) petitions, U visas, and employment authorization. Through the Cuban-Haitian Program, additional assistance is available for work authorization, parole, adjustment of status, and citizenship. 862 SE Oak Street Suite 3A Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Individuals who are refugees or immigrants Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 384-2482 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and representation on domestic abuse and family law matters. Information available in regard to immigration, public benefits, tenant/landlord disputes, discrimination law, and contested restraining orders. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assault Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home delivered meals, naturalization assistance, homebound assistance, limited transportation assistance, case management, health and wellness activities, and focal point leadership for older adults and their caregivers. An Information and Assistance specialist is available to provide information and referrals. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Some services have income eligibility criteria. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Meals: Varies Email (503) 484-6371 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IMMIGRATION AND COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides certified immigration assistance to immigrants and refugees, including permanent resident status adjustment, employment authorization, citizenship status, family reunification, and replacement of lost green cards. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Immigrants and refugees Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6537 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services Against Exploitation (SAE) serves victims and survivors of all forms of human trafficking with an emphasis on foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee victims and survivors. A team of trauma-informed advocates and staff provides culturally and linguistically responsive case management, immigration legal assistance, and community referrals. They meet survivors where they are and offer survivor-centered, wrap-around services throughout each survivor’s journey toward self-empowerment and liberation. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Survivors and victims of all forms of human trafficking. Culturally and linguistically specific services available for foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee survivors. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5:30pm Email (971) 312-9602 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home delivered meals, naturalization assistance, homebound assistance, limited transportation assistance, case management, health and wellness activities, and focal point leadership for older adults and their caregivers. An Information and Assistance specialist is available to provide information and referrals. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Some services have income eligibility criteria. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Meals: Varies (503) 484-6371 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE SERVICES AGAINST EXPLOITATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Services Against Exploitation (SAE) serves victims and survivors of all forms of human trafficking with an emphasis on foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee victims and survivors. A team of trauma-informed advocates and staff provides culturally and linguistically responsive case management, immigration legal assistance, and community referrals. They meet survivors where they are and offer survivor-centered, wrap-around services throughout each survivor’s journey toward self-empowerment and liberation. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Survivors and victims of all forms of human trafficking. Culturally and linguistically specific services available for foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee survivors. A team of trauma-informed advocates and staff provides culturally and linguistically responsive case management, immigration legal assistance, and community referrals. They meet survivors where they are and offer survivor-centered, wrap-around services throughout each survivor’s journey toward self-empowerment and liberation. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Survivors and victims of all forms of human trafficking. Culturally and linguistically specific services available for foreign-born, immigrant, and refugee survivors. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5:30pm Email (971) 312-9602 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO SENIOR SERVICES CENTER SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home delivered meals, naturalization assistance, homebound assistance, limited transportation assistance, case management, health and wellness activities, and focal point leadership for older adults and their caregivers. An Information and Assistance specialist is available to provide information and referrals. 10165 SE Cherry Blossom Drive Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Some services have income eligibility criteria. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Meals: Varies (503) 484-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT CONNECTION PDX IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal assistance with issues relating to immigration such as family and marriage-based petitions, naturalization petitions, legal permanent resident and employment authorization renewals, adjustment of status, Consular processing, the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program, Temporary Protected Status (TPS), non-immigrant visas, religious worker visas, and other related immigration services. 16700 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (971) 202-2073 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRATION COUNSELING SERVICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal services for immigration issues such as family visa petitions, permanent residency, citizenship applications, consular processing, representation in front of USCIS (formerly INS), renewal of work permits or resident cards, asylum, and defense against removal (deportation). Also provides assistance in obtaining U and T visas. 519 SW Park Avenue Suite 610 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Initial consultation: Unrestricted Representation by attorney or Accredited Representative: Income must be no higher than 200% of the federal poverty level Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm by appointment only Email (503) 221-1689 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INTERFAITH MOVEMENT FOR IMMIGRANT JUSTICE ACCOMPANIMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides accompaniment services for individuals attending ICE check-ins, immigration court hearting, Intensive Supervision Appearance Program appointments, and other mandatory dates during the immigration process. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 550-3510 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST BEAVERTON SAFE ROUTE IMMIGRATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with lawful permanent residency (Green Card), visa services, assistance in acquiring passports including passport photos, asylum services, translation and notary services, travel documents and re-entry permits, and other legal services. 3800 SW Cedar Hills Boulevard Suite 288 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Individuals who are refugees, asylees, or immigrants Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-2:30pm Email (503) 924-2488 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST PORTLAND OFFICE SAFE ROUTE IMMIGRATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with lawful permanent residency (Green Card), visa services, assistance in acquiring passports including passport photos, asylum services, translation and notary services, travel documents and re-entry permits, and other legal services. 7931 NE Halsey Street Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Individuals who are refugees, asylees, or immigrants Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-2:30pm Email (503) 731-9568 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST LUTHERAN COMMUNITY SERVICES NORTHWEST YAMHILL COUNTY SAFE ROUTE IMMIGRATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with lawful permanent residency (Green Card), visa services, assistance in acquiring passports including passport photos, asylum services, translation and notary services, travel documents and re-entry permits, and other legal services. 435 NE Evans Street Suite A McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Individuals who are refugees, asylees, or immigrants Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday by appointment Email (503) 472-4020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY DEPARTMENT OF COMMUNITY JUSTICE MULTNOMAH COUNTY COURTHOUSE U-VISA PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance, including providing information on eligibility criteria, connection with certification officials, and support and guidance in applying for the U-Visa program. This program allows eligible victims to temporarily stay in the United States and access necessary services while aiding law enforcment efforts. Victims have access to emergency medical care, access to police assistance, and community-based services geared towards increasing safety and well-being. 1200 SW 1st Avenue Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Individuals who are immigrants and victims of violent crimes, sexual crimes, felony or domestic crimes, coercion and exploitation attempts, or fraud Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-3222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NOWIA UNETE ECO NAVIGATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides referrals to immigration legal services including access to lawyers, legal clinics, financial assistance with filing fees, and general information and instruction about immigration law and immigrant rights. 607 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Households at or below 200% of the federal poverty level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Friday noon-3pm and 4pm-7pm Email (541) 245-1625 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON ROHINGYA MYANMAR MUSLIM SOCIETY LEGAL SERVICE SUPPORT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers legal service support program to help individuals and families access reliable, culturally informed legal assistance and immigration processes including green card applications, citizenship and naturalization, refugee/asylee adjustment, renewals, family petitions, and legal document preparation. The team works closely with immigration attorneys, interpreters, and community partners to ensure clients understand their rights and options. 2410 SE 121st Avenue Suite #208 Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Must meet one of the following: -Immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers -Low-income individuals and families residing in Oregon -Individuals with limited English proficiency -Families experiencing hardship, displacement, or housing instability -Youth, adults, and elders across all ages -BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, Persons of Color) -Rohingya and Myanmar Community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 489-5179 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON WORKER RELIEF EQUITY CORPS OF OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides immigration legal services including access to lawyers, legal clinics, financial assistance with filing fees, and general information and instruction about immigration law and immigrant rights. Eligibility: Household must be below 200% of the federal poverty level and at risk of deportation or need help with an immigration legal matter Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm Email (888) 274-7292 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE CASCADE CAMPUS SOAR IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES/PCC IMMIGRATION CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal services such as immigration screenings, representation for Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) renewals and Employment Authorization Document (EAD) applications, and guidance on Advance Parole eligibility and the application process. Offer workshops on DACA, Advance Parole, and other immigration law updates. Provides possible immigration options and referrals to legal partners for support. 705 N Killingsworth Portland, OR 97217 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Prioritizes Portland Community College (PCC) students. Hours: 2nd, 3rd, and 4th Thursday of the month 9am-4pm by appointment only Email (503) 379-0964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE ROCK CREEK CAMPUS SOAR IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES/PCC IMMIGRATION CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal services such as immigration screenings, representation for Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) renewals and Employment Authorization Document (EAD) applications, and guidance on Advance Parole eligibility and the application process. Offer workshops on DACA, Advance Parole, and other immigration law updates. Provides possible immigration options and referrals to legal partners for support. 17705 NW Springville Road Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Prioritizes Portland Community College (PCC) students. Hours: 1st Thursday of the month 9am-4pm by appointment only Email (503) 379-0964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PUEBLO UNIDO PDX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a navigation team that helps community members overcome financial, technological, linguistic, and literacy barriers to legal representation and social services. Eligibility: Individuals with vulnerable immigration status Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, closed federal holidays Email (503) 912-8925 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOMOS HISPANAS UNIDAS SILVERTON IMMIGRATION LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice and representation on immigration matters to individuals in immigration proceedings before the US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) Department of Homeland Security (DHS). 512 N 1st Street Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Income should not exceed the HHS Poverty Guidelines for Fee Waiver Request (USCIS Form I-912P) Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (503) 873-7114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED SERVICES FOR COUNSELING IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides counseling, representation, and advocacy for the following immigration matters: -Naturalization/citizenship -Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) -Adjustment to permanent resident status (green card) for parolees, refugees/asylees, and family-based adjustments -Replacement or renewal of permanent resident card (green card) -Fiancée petitions -Immigrant visa petitions for spouses, children, and siblings -Petition to remove conditional status -Travel document/re-entry permit/Advance Parole -Employment authorization document (work permit) -Replacement of I-94 Card (Arrival/Departure Card) -Extension of non-immigrant visas -Applications for waiver of grounds of inadmissibility and excludability -Refugee/asylee relative petitions -Requests for waiver of immigration application/immigration fees -Affidavits of Support -Accompaniment to interviews at U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) office Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 698-4929 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC CHARITIES IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES INC CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES
